app.name: weapp-tailwindcss
app.one_liner: A comprehensive solution to bring Tailwind CSS (v2–v4) into WeChat Mini Programs and cross-platform frameworks like uni-app x.

screens.name: Integration Dashboard
screens.purpose: Showcase supported frameworks, bundlers, and Tailwind versions with quick-start templates.

rust_commands.name: transpile_tailwind_classes
rust_commands.purpose: Process Tailwind CSS classes—including dynamic and arbitrary values—into static equivalents compatible with mini-program runtimes.

rust_commands.name: validate_bundler_config
rust_commands.purpose: Verify compatibility of the current bundler setup (webpack/Vite/etc.) with the selected Tailwind version.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add support for webpack and Vite integration
week 1: Create basic template for Taro and uni-app
week 1: Implement core PostCSS plugin for Tailwind v3/v4 class extraction
acceptance_tests: Arbitrary values such as `bg-[#000]` must persist through HMR and incremental builds without being dropped.
Cross-platform output (Web, Mini Program, iOS, Android, HarmonyOS) must be achievable via uni-app x configuration.
Dynamic class names like `gap-[20px]` must be correctly transpiled in JS contexts under Tailwind v4.
Space-x/y utilities must not trigger syntax errors when used with Tailwind v4.
